paragraphs: Abbreviations of Books of the Bible

Abd. = Abdias [Obadiah]
Acts
Ag. = Aggeus [Haggai]
Amos
Apoc. = Apocalypse [Revelation]
Col. = Colossians
Cor. = Corinthians
Dan. = Daniel
Deut. = Deuteronomy
Eccles. = Ecclesiastes
Ecclus. = Ecclesiasticus [Sirach]
Eph. = Ephesians
Esd. = Esdras [1 Esdras (Ezra); 2 Esdras (Nehemiah)]
Est. = Esther
Ex. = Exodus
Ezech = Ezechiel [Ezekiel]
Gal. = Galatians
Gen. = Genesis
Hab. = Habacuc [Habakkuk]
Heb. = Hebrews
Isa. = Isaias [Isaiah]
Jas. = James
Jer. = Jeremias [Jeremiah]
Job
Joel
John
Jon. = Jonas [Jonah]
Jos. = Josue [Joshua]
Jdgs. = Judges
Jude
Jud. = Judith
Kgs. = Kings [1 Kings (1 Samuel); 2 Kings (2 Samuel); 3 Kings (1 Kings); 4 Kings (2 Kings)]
Lam. = Lamentations
Lev. = Leviticus
Luke
Mach. = Machabees [Maccabees]
Mal. = Malachias [Malachi]
Mark
Matt. = Matthew
Mich. = Micheas [Micah]
Num. = Numbers
Os. = Osee [Hosea]
Par. = Paralipomenon [Chronicles]
Pet. = Peter
Philem. = Philemon
Phil. = Philippians
Prov. = Proverbs
Ps. = Psalm
Pss. = Psalms
Rom. = Romans
Ruth
Soph. = Sophonias [Zephaniah]
Thess. = Thessalonians
Tim. = Timothy
Titus
Tob. = Tobias [Tobit]
Wisd. = Wisdom
Zach. = Zacharias [Zechariah]
Note: In citing passages from the Bible, the chapter numbers are always given in italics, the verse numbers in usual arabic type.
[Transcriber's Note: Below is a list of other abbreviations and a key to biblical references that the reader might encounter in these works.
Other Abbreviations

A.D. = Anno Domini, year of the Lord.
B.C. = (year) Before Christ.
Cf. = Confer
cf. = confer
Cp. = Compare
cp. = compare
e.g. = for example
f = and following verse.
ff = and following verses.
i.e. = that is to say.
MS. = manuscript
MSS. = manuscripts
p. = page
pp. = pages
Sept. = Septuagint
St. = Saint
Sts. = Saints
V. = Verse
v. = verse
Vv. = Verses
vv. = verses
& = and
&c. = etc., et cetera, and so forth.
Key to Biblical References
Matt. 1, 5 refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5.
Matt. 1, 5a refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, the first part of verse 5.
Matt. 1, 5b refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, the second part of verse 5.
Matt. 1, 5 f refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5 and the following verse, i.e., verses 5 and 6.
Matt. 1, 5 ff refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5 and at least the following two verses, i.e., verses 5, 6 and 7.
Matt. 1, 5.10 refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5 and verse 10.
Matt. 1, 5.10 f refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5, verse 10 and the following verse, i.e., verses 5, 10 and 11.
Matt. 1, 5-10 refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verses 5 to 10 inclusive
Matt. 1, 5; 2, 3 refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5 and chapter 2, verse 3.
Matt. 1, 5 -- 2, 3 refers to the Book of Matthew, chapter 1, verse 5 to chapter 2, verse 3 inclusive.

Note: Unless the context suggests otherwise, a verse number appearing by itself refers to the same chapter and book currently under consideration and a chapter number appearing by itself refers to the same book currently under consideration.
Numbering of the Psalms

The Confraternity Bible uses a system for numbering the Psalms that is slightly different from the numbering system typically used in more modern Bibles. The following table is included to help facilitate navigation between the two systems.
